_notes: Evaluation dataset containing both synthetic and real contract samples. Synthetic examples are reserved for pipeline validation only. PII has been removed from all real documents.

clauses.text: Landlord may adjust rent with seven (7) days written notice to Tenant.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: 7-day notice is well below the typical 30-day standard for rent increases

clauses.text: A refundable pet deposit of $300 is required for tenants with approved pets.
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Standard, in line with typical local terms

clauses.text: Early termination requires payment of a fee calculated per the formula in Exhibit C, section 2, subject to adjustment.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Fee formula is vague and cross-references another section, making the actual cost unclear

clauses.text: Tenant may request repairs at any time via written notice, and Landlord will respond within 48 hours for non-emergency issues.
clauses.label: favorable
clauses.reason: Clear, tenant-friendly response time commitment, stronger than typical

clauses.text: Security deposit will be returned within 21 days of move-out, less any deductions for damages.
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Standard timeline, matches most state requirements

clauses.text: Tenant waives the right to a jury trial in any dispute arising from this agreement.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Jury trial waiver significantly limits tenant's legal options and is easy to miss

clauses.text: Landlord shall maintain the property in compliance with all applicable health and safety codes.
clauses.label: favorable
clauses.reason: Explicit maintenance obligation protects the tenant

clauses.text: Claims must be filed within 30 days of the incident or coverage may be denied.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: 30-day window is short and denial-on-technicality risk is high if missed

clauses.text: Pre-existing conditions disclosed at enrollment are covered after a 90-day waiting period.
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Standard waiting period structure, clearly disclosed

clauses.text: The insurer reserves the right to adjust premiums annually based on claims history, with 60 days written notice.
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Reasonable notice period, standard practice

documents.source: eforms.com PA Standard Lease Agreement (blank template)

clauses.text: If the Premises includes a washer, dryer, freezer, dehumidifier unit and/or air conditioning unit, the Landlord makes no warranty as to the repair or replacement of units if one or all shall fail to operate.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Landlord has no obligation to fix or replace major appliances if they break — easy to miss since it reads like a routine maintenance clause

clauses.text: Guests of the Tenant are allowed for periods not lasting for more than 48 hours unless otherwise approved by the Landlord in writing.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: 48-hour guest limit is restrictive and easy to violate unknowingly without realizing it's a lease term

clauses.text: Tenant, or any Occupant(s) is arrested, convicted, or given deferred adjudication for a criminal offense involving actual or potential physical harm to a person...regardless of whether arrest or conviction occurs.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Default can be triggered by arrest alone, without any conviction — an unusually low bar that most tenants wouldn't expect

clauses.text: The Landlord shall not be liable for any damage or injury to the Tenant...unless caused solely by the Landlord's negligence.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Shifts nearly all liability risk onto the tenant; 'solely' is a high bar that's hard for a tenant to prove

clauses.text: The Landlord is prohibited from making any type of retaliatory acts against the Tenant including but not limited to restricting access to the Premises, decreasing or cancelling services or utilities, failure to repair appliances or fixtures.
clauses.label: favorable
clauses.reason: Explicit tenant protection against landlord retaliation, stronger than many leases include

clauses.text: If the Tenant possesses any mental or physical impairment, the Landlord shall provide reasonable modifications to the Premises unless the modifications would be too difficult or expensive for the Landlord to provide.
clauses.label: favorable
clauses.reason: Clear accommodation commitment beyond baseline legal requirement framing

clauses.text: The Landlord shall have the right to enter the Premises during normal working hours by providing at least twenty-four (24) hours notice.
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Standard notice period, matches typical market practice

clauses.text: If the Tenant vacates or abandons the Premises for a time-period that is the minimum set by State law or seven (7) days, whichever is less, the Landlord shall have the right to terminate this Agreement immediately.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: 7 days is a short window before the landlord can treat the unit as abandoned and remove belongings

documents.source: Consumer.gov / FTC Sample Rental Agreement (US government work, public domain)

clauses.text: In the event rent is not received prior to the 4th of the month, Tenant agrees to pay a $25 late fee, plus an additional $5 per day for every day thereafter until the rent is paid. Neither ill health, loss of job, financial emergency, or other excuses will be accepted for late payment.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Escalating daily late fee with no exceptions for genuine hardship is unusually harsh and uncapped

clauses.text: No interest will be paid on this money and in no case will it be applied to back or future rent. It will be held intact by Landlord until at least thirty (30) working days after Tenants have vacated.
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: No-interest deposits are standard in most states; 30 working days plus review is within normal range

clauses.text: They agree to return the property in the same condition or pay a $200.00 minimum cleaning fee if the Landlord has to have the property professionally cleaned.
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Flat, disclosed cleaning fee is standard and not hidden

clauses.text: Tenants agree to keep a maximum of 1 vehicle on premises or in the garage.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Single-vehicle limit is restrictive and easy to overlook when signing, especially for multi-adult households

clauses.text: If the tenant does not notify landlord in a prompt matter the tenant may be held financially responsible.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Vague 'prompt' standard with real financial consequence attached — no defined timeframe for what counts as compliant

clauses.text: If anyone removes any property belonging to Landlord without the express written consent of the Landlord, this will constitute abandonment and surrender of the premises by Tenant.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Broadly worded — could be triggered unintentionally and treated as full lease abandonment

clauses.text: Upon expiration, this Agreement shall become a month-to-month agreement AUTOMATICALLY, UNLESS either Tenants or Owners notify the other party in writing at least 30 days prior to expiration.
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Standard auto-renewal structure with reasonable, symmetric notice period for both parties

clauses.text: This lease automatically renews under the same terms...with a $100.00 per month rental increase unless another lease, an addendum and/or a lease extension is executed, or written notice of non-renewal is given 120 days before expiration. The right to renew this lease is entirely at the discretion of the Landlord.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Automatic renewal with a guaranteed rent increase, a 120-day notice window most tenants wouldn't track, and renewal being entirely the landlord's discretion despite the tenant carrying the notice burden

clauses.text: If Tenant breaks this lease, Tenant agrees to give up his right of a "Notice to Quit". This means Tenant allows Landlord to go to court without giving the required notice.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Waives a standard legal protection tenants normally have before being taken to court

clauses.text: If Tenant breaks this lease in any way, the deposit is not refundable.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Extremely broad forfeiture condition — any lease violation, however minor, voids the entire deposit

clauses.text: If we continue to attempt to contact you for 3 full days and we do not receive a response from you, you will be charged a $100.00 fee. NO EXCEPTIONS!
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: A real fee tied to not answering the phone within 3 days, with no exceptions carve-out for any circumstance

clauses.text: Landlord has the right to enter the leased property at any time without advance notice. Landlord...has the right to inspect, show, make repairs, and do maintenance even if the Tenant is not at home.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: No-notice entry for routine (non-emergency) purposes is well outside the typical 24-hour notice standard

clauses.text: Tenant is required to purchase personal liability insurance ($100,000). Failure to maintain personal liability insurance is an incurable breach of this Lease Contract and may result in the termination of tenancy and eviction.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Mandatory insurance framed as an 'incurable breach' — lapsing coverage even briefly could trigger eviction with no cure period

clauses.text: If tenant or any guest or occupant violates animal restrictions (with or without tenants knowledge), tenant will be subject to charges ($500 per animal, recurring monthly).
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Recurring monthly $500 charge, and explicitly applies even if the tenant had no knowledge of the violation

clauses.text: No smoking or vaping inside the property. If you are found in violation of this rule, you will be charged a $500.00 fine...Landlord will use own discretion based off of observed condition of property to determine if smoking or vaping has occurred.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: The fine is triggered by the landlord's own subjective discretion, with no independent verification standard

clauses.text: Tenant or Landlord may agree to submit any dispute concerning this lease to final and binding mediation/arbitration. Both the Tenant and the landlord choose the mediator.
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Requires mutual agreement and joint mediator selection, more balanced than a unilateral arbitration clause

clauses.text: We will hold the security deposit for the term of the tenancy and, upon termination of the tenancy, reserve the right to use the security deposit...to cover any charges related to your performance of this Lease Contract.
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Fairly standard security deposit usage language

clauses.text: Tenant agrees to give up his right of a "Notice to Quit"...Landlord may report any past rent, damages, utilities or other costs owed by Tenant to a credit reporting agency.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Credit reporting exposure disclosed, but combined with other clauses here it adds another real financial consequence tenants may not register while signing

clauses.text: Specific content can be deleted without reason and may be removed without prior notice
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: ToS;DR blocker-level: no cause required and no warning given before your content is removed

clauses.text: You waive your moral rights
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: ToS;DR blocker-level: giving up rights over how your own content/name is used or attributed

clauses.text: Your personal data is used for automated decision-making, profiling or AI training
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: ToS;DR blocker-level: your data can train AI systems or drive automated decisions about you

clauses.text: You are forced into binding arbitration in case of disputes
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Removes your ability to take a dispute to court

clauses.text: You waive your right to a class action.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: You can't join a group lawsuit against the service, only pursue disputes individually

clauses.text: Instead of asking directly, this Service will assume your consent to changes of terms merely from your usage.
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Continuing to use the app counts as agreeing to changed terms you may never have seen

clauses.text: Any liability on behalf of the service is only limited to the fees you paid as a user (or $30 USD)
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Caps what you could ever recover from the service at a very low fixed amount

clauses.text: Your content can be licensed to third parties
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: What you upload isn't just used by the service itself — it can be passed to other companies

clauses.text: You can request access, correction and/or deletion of your data
clauses.label: favorable
clauses.reason: Real, actionable data rights for the user

clauses.text: You have the right to leave this service at any time
clauses.label: favorable
clauses.reason: No lock-in on account termination

clauses.text: You maintain ownership of your content
clauses.label: favorable
clauses.reason: You retain ownership even though it can also be licensed out (see content_licensing_to_third_parties) — worth reading both together

clauses.text: The terms may be changed at any time, but you will receive notification of the changes
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Standard change-of-terms clause, but with real notice, which is the fairer version of this pattern

clauses.text: This service has a no refund policy with some exceptions
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Common industry standard, not unusually restrictive as written

clauses.text: They store data on you even if you did not interact with the service
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: ToS;DR blocker-level: data collection isn't limited to people who actually use the service

clauses.text: Your identity is used in ads that are shown to other users
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: ToS;DR blocker-level: your name/likeness can appear in advertising shown to other people without a separate opt-in described here

clauses.text: Private messages that are not end-to-end encrypted can be read
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: ToS;DR blocker-level: messages you'd reasonably assume are private aren't cryptographically protected from the service itself

clauses.text: Your personal data is used for automated decision-making, profiling or AI training
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: ToS;DR blocker-level: same pattern as Spotify, your data trains systems or drives automated decisions about you

clauses.text: Deleted content is not really deleted
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: ToS;DR blocker-level: clicking delete doesn't mean the data is actually gone

clauses.text: This service may collect, use, and share location data, even if you turn off location sharing
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Turning off the location toggle doesn't reliably stop location collection

clauses.text: This service tracks you on other websites
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Tracking extends beyond the platform itself into your browsing elsewhere

clauses.text: Your biometric data is collected
clauses.label: concerning
clauses.reason: Biometric data is an especially sensitive category with real re-identification risk if leaked

clauses.text: You aren't forced into binding arbitration in case of disputes
clauses.label: favorable
clauses.reason: Preserves your normal right to go to court, unlike Spotify's arbitration clause

clauses.text: When the service wants to make a material change to its terms, you are notified at least 30 days in advance
clauses.label: favorable
clauses.reason: Concrete, specific notice period rather than a vague 'you'll be notified' promise

clauses.text: This service does not sell your personal data
clauses.label: favorable
clauses.reason: Explicit commitment, notable given how much data collection is flagged elsewhere on this same page

clauses.text: You maintain ownership of your content
clauses.label: favorable
clauses.reason: Same pattern as Spotify, ownership retained even though usage rights are licensed out

clauses.text: Users who have been permanently banned from this service are not allowed to re-register under a new account
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Standard platform enforcement term

clauses.text: The service assumes no liability for any damages the user incurs
clauses.label: neutral
clauses.reason: Boilerplate liability disclaimer, common across most services including higher-graded ones
